prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='catboost', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7182) and high seasonality variance (0.6712), with early horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, CWT_rf) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_svr, DWT_svr). catboost best captures the upward trend while ARIMA excels at seasonal patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors across horizons and capture different decomposition components.

You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_catboost", "late": "ONLY_CWT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "ETS", "THETA", "CWT_svr", "DWT_rf", "catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "rf", "CWT_rf", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "NaiveSeasonal", "FT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.7182,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7182) and high seasonality variance (0.6712), with early horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, CWT_rf) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_svr, DWT_svr). catboost best captures the upward trend while ARIMA excels at seasonal patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors across horizons and capture different decomposition components."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.7182) and high seasonality variance (0.6712), with early horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, CWT_rf) differing from late horizon specialists (ONLY_CWT_svr, DWT_svr). catboost best captures the upward trend while ARIMA excels at seasonal patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors across horizons and capture different decomposition components.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.7182 (best ONLY_CWT_svr=8304 vs worst ONLY_FT_catboost=14167) justifies topk_mean with small k and shrinkage. pattern_analyst_insights explicitly recommends dba_combination for high seasonality variance (0.6712) and model disagreement. With n_windows=3, conservative top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.35 prevent overfitting.
